idea.hook: What's the OLDEST light you can see? 🤯🌌
facts: This light was released ~380,000 years AFTER the Big Bang.
It has traveled ~13.8 BILLION years across the universe to reach your eyes.
idea.payoff: This faint glow is literally the universe's 'baby picture'!
idea.title: Universe's Baby Photo! 👶✨🌌

scenes.visual: Starts with a deep, dark cosmic background with a few distant, faint stars. A subtle, swirling galaxy animation might be in the very background, barely visible.
The dark background shifts to show an abstract, slightly textured cosmic pattern, hints of reds and blues, representing a 'soup' of early universe energy. A subtle, very faint glowing haze appears.

scenes.text: Released ~380,000 years AFTER the Big Bang.
scenes.visual: The background brightens briefly as if a flash, then expands outwards, showing a smooth, uniform yellowish-white glow, signifying the early, hot universe becoming transparent.

scenes.text: Traveled ~13.8 BILLION years to reach you!
scenes.visual: A long, arcing dotted line (representing light's path) shoots across a vast cosmic landscape filled with distant galaxies and nebulae, implying immense distance and time.
The iconic all-sky map of the CMB (the oval, mottled image with hot and cold spots) slowly rotates or zooms out slightly, filling the screen with its vibrant, ancient data.
